extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won the sprint in Orbetello but was disqualified for deviation, handing GAVIRIA Fernando the stage victory.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ Primož lacks technical positioning experience, dropped to 30-40th in Grosseto's roundabouts despite pink jersey.
ACKERMANN Pascal launched sprint early into headwind with 350m remaining, completely gassed before 100m mark.
DÉMARE Arnaud targeted both intermediate sprints unopposed while rival sprint trains conserved energy for finale.
VIVIANI Elia abandoned SABATINI leadout mid-sprint to jump ACKERMANN wheel, tactical switch enabled winning acceleration.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: Pink jersey but exposed poor bunch positioning skills in technical urban sections, lost teammates easily
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: Won Stage 2 but tactical naivety shown launching 350m out into headwind, completely empty at 100m
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: Won sprint then disqualified for deviation, but demonstrated superior timing leaving acceleration to sub-100m
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: positive: Strong late acceleration, immediately competitive after early-season struggles, showed good sportsmanship pre-DQ news
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: Opportunistic double intermediate sprint wins while rivals saved legs, team mopped secondary points efficiently
